label: Auroraglass Scriptorium Cell
description: Auroraglass prisms set high in the basalt wall admit narrow bands of bruise-violet light, cutting across a slab-built sleeping niche and a recessed lectern fixed in slagcrete. Copper grounding straps lace the ceiling and descend to a floor grate, their riveted runs designed to bleed electrostatic charge into the temple’s insulated lattice. The furnishings remain austere and permanent: a stone plinth for effects, a wall niche for devotional texts, and a shuttered vox-silence vent packed with ash baffles. The chamber’s surfaces carry a permanent film of soot, sealed under waxed sealant that resists Ashenward City’s abrasive gales.
